changes.summary: Update Pydantic v2 code to address deprecations
changes.details: When running `ty` on the code base (cf #15091), we got two valid deprecation warnings: - `eval_type_lenient` has been deprecated since [pydantic#1053](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/pull/10530), which was released as part of [v2.10.0b1](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/releases/tag/v2.10.0b1).…

changes.summary: Validate Server Sent Event fields to avoid applications from sending broken data
changes.details: Validate Server Sent Event fields to avoid applications from sending broken data This was reported multiple times as a "vulnerability" by users probably using automated AI tools, with the argument that if input was taken from the request and passed directly to an event unfiltered, it could break the client.…

changes.summary: Do not accept underscore headers when using `convert_underscores=True` (the default)
changes.details: Do not accept underscore headers when using `convert_underscores=True` (the default) This means it would only accept header names separated with hyphens by default, and to accept header names separated with underscores, an app would need to explicitly set `convert_underscores=False`.

changes.summary: Refactor internals to preserve `APIRouter` and `APIRoute` instances
changes.details: Refactor internals to preserve `APIRouter` and `APIRoute` instances Supersedes https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/4794 Unblocks :sparkles: SO MANY THINGS :sparkles: Before this, `router.include_router(other_router)` would take each path operation from `other_router` and "clone" it, or recreate it from scratch.…

changes.summary: Fix bug, allow empty path in path operation in prefixless router
changes.details: Fix bug, allow empty path in path operation in prefixless router Related to https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues/15762
Fix typing checks for APIRoute Related to: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues/15764

changes.summary: Add `iter_route_contexts()` for advanced use cases that used to use `router.routes` (e.g. Jupyverse)
changes.details: Add `iter_route_contexts()` for advanced use cases that used to use `router.routes` (e.g. Jupyverse) Related to: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/discussions/15782 Codex with GPT 5.5 through several iterations, defining and trimming the interface. Manual review of all the code.

changes.summary: Routine maintenance: 151 translation, dependency, and internal PRs
changes.details: Aggregated translation, dependency-bump, CI, and internal-tooling pull requests with no linked tracking ticket. Collapsed into one entry so the change set foregrounds substantive work; the full list is preserved in unlinkedArtifactIds.

reasons: Breaking change: "Refactor internals to preserve `APIRouter` and `APIRoute` instances" (pr:15745) — affects a core API; downstream code may need migration.
2 fixes clustered in "routing" (pr:15763, pr:15765) suggest a recently-changed area shipped with rough edges.
1 dependency/upgrade change(s) (e.g. pr:15101) can shift transitive behavior.
Wide blast radius: 7 components touched (compat, dependencies, encoders, sse, applications, openapi, …).

changelog.text: `include_router()` now preserves `APIRouter` and `APIRoute` instances instead of copying them. Routers keep their identity when composed, and `router.routes` is now a tree rather than a flat list — code that iterated it expecting a flat list of routes may need to migrate to `iter_route_contexts()`.

Added `iter_route_contexts()` — the supported way to walk a router's routes for advanced introspection (e.g. tooling like Jupyverse), replacing direct access to the now-tree-structured `router.routes`.
Header parameters with the default `convert_underscores=True` no longer accept underscores in header names, removing an ambiguity in how underscore/dash headers were matched.
Server-Sent Event fields are now validated before being sent, so applications can no longer emit malformed SSE payloads.
Fixed an empty path in a path operation on a prefix-less router.
Updated internal usage of Pydantic v2 to clear deprecation warnings and stay current with the v2 API.
Routine maintenance: translation updates, dependency bumps, and internal/CI changes (151 PRs, summarized rather than itemized).

internalReleaseNotes.heading: Overview
internalReleaseNotes.body: The headline of this release is a refactor of FastAPI's routing internals: `include_router()` now preserves `APIRouter`/`APIRoute` instances instead of recreating them, so route identity is retained and `router.routes` becomes a tree rather than a flat list. Shipping alongside it are two routing fixes, a new `iter_route_contexts()` introspection API, a stricter header-conversion rule, SSE field validation, and a Pydantic v2 deprecation cleanup.

internalReleaseNotes.heading: Affected systems
internalReleaseNotes.body: routing, applications, and openapi (the router refactor and `iter_route_contexts()`); dependencies (the header-conversion change); sse (SSE validation); compat / encoders (the Pydantic v2 cleanup).

internalReleaseNotes.heading: Risk — High
internalReleaseNotes.body: Driven by the breaking routing refactor on a core API (downstream code may need migration), two fixes clustered in the recently-changed routing area suggesting rough edges, a dependency upgrade that can shift transitive behavior, and a wide blast radius across seven components. Recommend extra regression coverage around router composition and any code that introspects routes.

internalReleaseNotes.heading: Migration notes
internalReleaseNotes.body: Replace any direct iteration of `router.routes` (now a tree) with `iter_route_contexts()`. Applications that relied on underscores being accepted in header names under the default `convert_underscores=True` must adjust their header definitions.

internalReleaseNotes.heading: Coverage & data quality
internalReleaseNotes.body: All 7 tracked tickets (FAPI-1000…1006) are represented above. The remaining ~151 PRs in the window are translation, dependency-bump, and internal/CI work with no linked ticket; they were summarized as one Maintenance entry rather than itemized, and surfaced as unlinked artifacts rather than dropped silently.

customerReleaseNotes.heading: What's new
customerReleaseNotes.body: Router handling is now more robust and predictable: routers keep their identity when you compose them with other routers, which makes larger, more modular applications easier to build. A new supported way to inspect an application's routes is available for advanced tooling.

customerReleaseNotes.heading: Improvements
customerReleaseNotes.body: Streaming responses are validated before they're sent, so malformed server-sent events can't slip through, and header handling is now stricter and less ambiguous.

customerReleaseNotes.heading: Action required
customerReleaseNotes.body: If your code reads an application's internal list of routes directly, switch to the new supported inspection method. If you rely on underscores in custom header names, review your header definitions, as underscores are no longer accepted by default.

documentationUpdates.suggestion: Update the "Automatic conversion" section: with the default `convert_underscores=True`, header names that contain underscores are no longer accepted. Document the new, stricter behavior and note that setting `convert_underscores=False` opts out of conversion entirely.

Update the "Include the `APIRouter`s" section: `include_router()` now preserves `APIRouter`/`APIRoute` instances instead of copying them, so `router.routes` is a tree rather than a flat list. Note that advanced code which introspected `router.routes` should move to the new `iter_route_contexts()` API.

retrieval.text:
### [APIStar](https://github.com/encode/apistar) (<= 0.5) { #apistar-0-5 }

Right before deciding to build **FastAPI** I found **APIStar** server. It had almost everything I was looking for and had a great design.

It was one of the first implementations of a framework using Python type hints to declare parameters and requests that I ever saw (before NestJS and Molten). I found it more or less at the same time as Hug. But APIStar used the OpenAPI standard.

It had automatic data validation, data serialization and OpenAPI schema generation based on the same type hints in several places.

Body schema definitions didn't use the same Python type hints like Pydantic, it was a bit more similar to Marshmallow, so, editor support wouldn't be as good, but still, APIStar was the best available option.

It had the best performance benchmarks at the time (only surpassed by Starlette).

At first, it didn't have an automatic API documentation web UI, but I knew I could add Swagger UI to it.

It had a dependency injection system. It required pre-registration of components, as other tools discussed above. But still, it was a great feature.

I was never able to use it in a full project, as it didn't have security integration, so, I couldn't replace all the features I was having with the full-stack generators based on Flask-apispec. I had in my backlog of projects to create a pull request adding that functionality.

But then, the project's focus shifted.

It was no longer an API web framework, as the creator needed to focus on Starlette.

Now APIStar is a set of tools to validate OpenAPI specifications, not a web framework.

/// info

APIStar was created by Tom Christie. The same guy that created:

* Django REST Framework
* Starlette (in which **FastAPI** is based)
* Uvicorn (used by Starlette and **FastAPI**)

///

/// check | Inspired **FastAPI** to

Exist.

The idea of declaring multiple things (data validation, serialization and documentation) with the same Python types, that at the same time provided great editor support, was something I considered a brilliant idea.

And after searching for a long time for a similar framework and testing many different alternatives, APIStar was the best option available.

Then APIStar stopped to exist as a server and Starlette was created, and was a new better foundation for such a system. That was the final inspiration to build **FastAPI**.

I consider **FastAPI** a "spiritual successor" to APIStar, while improving and increasing the features, typing system, and other parts, based on the learnings from all these previous tools.

///

### [Swagger](https://swagger.io/) / [OpenAPI](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/) { #swagger-openapi }

The main feature I wanted from Django REST Framework was the automatic API documentation.

Then I found that there was a standard to document APIs, using JSON (or YAML, an extension of JSON) called Swagger.

And there was a web user interface for Swagger APIs already created. So, being able to generate Swagger documentation for an API would allow using this web user interface automatically.

At some point, Swagger was given to the Linux Foundation, to be renamed OpenAPI.

That's why when talking about version 2.0 it's common to say "Swagger", and for version 3+ "OpenAPI".

/// check | Inspired **FastAPI** to

Adopt and use an open standard for API specifications, instead of a custom schema.

And integrate standards-based user interface tools:

* [Swagger UI](https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-ui)
* [ReDoc](https://github.com/Rebilly/ReDoc)

These two were chosen for being fairly popular and stable, but doing a quick search, you could find dozens of alternative user interfaces for OpenAPI (that you can use with **FastAPI**).

///
